meta.title: Microsyntax preprocessor
meta.subject: A demo of the R6 preprocessSpec hook. The film below renders normally; the proof is that the @example/docent-feature-microsyntax feature is registered and its preprocessSpec runs in the cascade.

scenes.title: Microsyntax
scenes.heading: Tighter spec, same render
scenes.footnote: FeaturePlugin.preprocessSpec — R6 forward-compat. Identity by default.

beats.narration: The microsyntax feature is registered. Its preprocessSpec hook ran before this film validated and rendered.
Three directives ship in the demo pack: @@@auto-id derives stable ids from labels; @@@reveal-all auto-fills a beat's reveal array; @@@beat-stride splits a long narration into N beats by sentence.
Identity is the default. A scene without a directives array passes through unchanged.

scenes.heading: What the preprocessor bought
points: Tighter source — authors write labels; ids are derived. No id collisions across renames.
Author shortcuts compile down to the canonical spec before the validator sees them.
Multiple features can chain preprocessSpec — the cascade runs them in registration order.

beats.narration: Spec stays tight. The validator sees the expanded form.
Multiple features can chain. Identity is the default.
Preprocessor is the R6 surface that holds it.
